records.ety: [L. diminutio, or perh. rather deminutio: cf. F. diminution. See Diminish.]

defs: The act of diminishing, or of making or becoming less; state of being diminished; reduction in size, quantity, or degree; -- opposed to augmentation or increase.
The act of lessening dignity or consideration, or the state of being deprived of dignity; a lowering in estimation; degradation; abasement.
quotes: The world's opinion or diminution of me.
Nor thinks it diminution to be ranked In military honor next.

defs: Omission, inaccuracy, or defect in a record.
In counterpoint, the imitation of, or reply to, a subject, in notes of half the length or value of those the subject itself.
